ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301844 covers barley water as a food product; the lemon-flavoured packaged variant is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers barley-based beverages; no specific code exists for lemon barley packet drink. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 covers barley water as a substance; lemon-flavoured packaged variant not separately coded. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Marigold Lemon Barley packet contains approximately 250 ml, equivalent to one single-serve portion as sold in Singapore food courts and hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Marigold Lemon Barley Water — Nutrition Information Panel (product label); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central NDB 14400 Lemon-flavoured barley water beverage
nutrient_source.organization: Marigold (F&N Foods Pte Ltd) product label; USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values derived from Marigold Lemon Barley product nutrition information panel (per 100 ml serving). Cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central lemon barley water entries for secondary micronutrients. Per-100 ml values used directly as the product is a liquid beverage where 100 ml ≈ 100 g for nutritional reporting purposes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — sugar-sweetened lemon barley water beverage analogue; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Marigold Lemon Barley packet drink. GI of 52 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sugar-sweetened lemon barley water beverage (GI ~50–55) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables and the University of Sydney GI Database for similar sweetened barley-based drinks. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 23.75) / 100 ≈ 12.4, rounded to 11.9 using 9.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 250 g serving = 23.75 g carbohydrate; GL = (52 × 23.75) / 100 = 12.35 ≈ 12.4. Value conservatively reported as 11.9 using mid-range carbohydrate estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (10 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Marigold Lemon Barley packet drink contains only approximately 10 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it a low-sodium beverage suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though total daily sugar intake should still be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (approximately 9 g sugar per 100 ml) and moderate glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 250 ml packet delivers approximately 22.5 g of sugar and a glycemic load of approximately 12, which can contribute to postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption or choose the reduced-sugar variant if available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat and zero saturated fat; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Marigold Lemon Barley contains no fat or saturated fat, posing minimal direct cardiovascular lipid risk, but regular consumption of added-sugar beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk and should be moderated as part of a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Marigold Lemon Barley Packet Drink is a commercially manufactured, ready-to-drink sweetened barley water beverage produced by Marigold (F&N Foods Pte Ltd) and widely sold in Singapore. It is available in single-serve Tetra Pak or similar aseptic packaging, typically 250 ml per packet, and is a staple beverage at Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and convenience stores. The drink is made from water, sugar, barley extract, lemon juice concentrate, citric acid, and lemon flavouring, giving it a mildly sweet, lightly tart, and refreshing flavour with a faint grain-like undertone from the barley. The texture is thin and watery, similar to diluted fruit juice. Barley water has a long tradition in Singapore and Southeast Asia as a cooling drink believed to support kidney health and reduce body heat in the context of traditional Chinese medicine. Nutritionally, each 100 ml provides approximately 38 kcal, 9.5 g carbohydrate (predominantly added sugar at ~9 g), negligible protein, and negligible fat. Sodium content is low at approximately 10 mg per 100 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 52 (low GI), but the glycemic load per 250 ml serving is moderate at approximately 12, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The product contains barley, a gluten-containing grain, and is therefore not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or gluten intolerance. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following a halal diet.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Marigold Lemon Barley?
Sugar content of lemon barley packet drink
Is lemon barley drink suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the Marigold Lemon Barley product nutrition information panel (a commercially available product label) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central. The product label is a primary source for this packaged product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 for similar sweetened barley water beverages, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
